turns.user_message: I've finally accepted that I'm just naturally bad at holding onto good things. It happens every single time something starts looking up for me. I'll get comfortable for a few days and then my own brain just decides to pick everything apart. I end up saying the wrong things or pulling back completely, and it feels completely predictable now. I just sit there watching it happen and wonder why I even bother trying to keep things steady.

It's been going on for years now, and I can't seem to break the cycle no matter what I try. Every time I let my guard down, I end up making some careless mistake that ruins the whole vibe. I replay the interactions in my head until I can clearly see exactly where I went wrong. The guilt just piles up until I feel completely heavy and useless. I keep telling myself I'll do better next time, but the pattern just repeats itself over and over.

I've reached the point where I just assume I'm going to mess everything up before it even starts. It's exhausting to carry this constant weight of knowing I'm the common denominator in all my own disappointments. I watch myself sabotage the good moments and I can't even muster the energy to pretend I'm surprised anymore. The shame sits right in my chest and makes it hard to even look at what I've done. I'm just tired of being the reason everything falls apart, but I don't know how to stop it.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a conclusion they've reached about themselves. This has been going on for a while.
